perturbation.original_text: 1. Definitions ​ . The following terms, as used herein, shall have the following meanings: (a) “ ​ Affiliate ​ ” means a Person that directly, or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controls, or is controlled by, or is under common control with, the Person specified of a Party. (b) “ ​ Confidential ​ Information ​ ” means all information, whether visual, written, electronic or oral, (including any information furnished prior to the execution of this Agreement) related to the products, services, technology, business, customers, working capital, affairs, assets, condition (financial or otherwise) or prospects of a Disclosing Party which is furnished by such Disclosing Party, or otherwise disclosed by such Disclosing Party, to a Receiving Party in connection with the consideration and evaluation of the Transaction (including, without limitation, samples, compounds and ingredients thereof, prototypes, formulae, technical know how, technical data, financial information, payment terms, performance data, product specifications and customer and/or supplier information) and all reports, analyses, compilations, data, studies and other materials (in whatever form maintained, whether documentary, computer storage or otherwise) UNITED STATES ​ | 545 W. Lake St. | Elmhurst, IL 60126 | 800.831.7294 MEXICO ​ | Nicolas Gogol #11332-A | Complejo Industrial Chihuahua | Chihuahua, Chih. 31136 www.chiefent.com Confidentiality Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 Page 1 of 6 Rev. 25-Feb-2020 prepared by the Receiving Party containing or reflecting such furnished information. All such Information shall be identified as confidential and/or proprietary in accordance with the following: (i) in the case of written or electronic information, such Information shall be identified at the time of disclosure with an appropriate legend, marking, stamp, or positive written identification on the face thereof or by an accompanying written statement; (ii) in the case of visual information, such Information shall be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or as soon thereafter as is reasonably practicable; and (iii) with respect to oral information, such information shall be reduced to written or language form, marked as confidential with an appropriate legend, marking, stamp, or positive written identification, and transmitted to the Receiving Party within thirty (30) days following the oral disclosure. The term “ ​ Confidential Information ​ ” does not include information (i) which is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Receiving Party or anyone to whom the Receiving Party transmits any Information, (ii) which is or becomes known or available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis and not in contravention of applicable law from a source (other than the Disclosing Party) which has represented to the Receiving Party (which the Receiving Party has no reason to disbelieve after due inquiry) that it is entitled to disclose it to the Receiving Party on such basis or (iii) which a Receiving Party can prove (1) was lawfully in its possession at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party or (2) was developed by the Receiving Party independently of any Information received from the Disclosing Party and without otherwise violating any of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definitions ​ . The following terms, as used herein, shall have the following meanings: (a) “ ​ Affiliate ​ ” means a Person that directly, or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controls, or is controlled by, or is under common control with, the Person specified of a Party. (b) “ ​ Confidential ​ Information ​ ” means all information, whether visual, written, electronic or oral, (including any information furnished prior to the execution of this Agreement) related to the products, services, technology, business, customers, working capital, affairs, assets, condition (financial or otherwise) or prospects of a Disclosing Party which is furnished by such Disclosing Party, or otherwise disclosed by such Disclosing Party, to a Receiving Party in connection with the consideration and evaluation of the Transaction (including, without limitation, samples, compounds and ingredients thereof, prototypes, formulae, technical know how, technical data, financial information, payment terms, performance data, product specifications and customer and/or supplier information) and all reports, analyses, compilations, data, studies and other materials (in whatever form maintained, whether documentary, computer storage or otherwise) prepared by the Receiving Party containing or reflecting such furnished information. All such Information shall be identified as confidential and/or proprietary in accordance with the following: (i) in the case of written or electronic information, such Information shall be identified at the time of disclosure with an appropriate legend, marking, stamp, or positive written identification on the face thereof or by an accompanying written statement; (ii) in the case of visual information, such Information shall be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or as soon thereafter as is reasonably practicable; and (iii) with respect to oral information, such information shall be reduced to written or language form, marked as confidential with an appropriate legend, marking, stamp, or positive written identification, and transmitted to the Receiving Party within thirty (30) days following the oral disclosure. The term “ ​ Confidential Information ​ ” does not include information (i) which is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Receiving Party or anyone to whom the Receiving Party transmits any Information, (ii) which is or becomes known or available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis from a source (other than the Disclosing Party) or (iii) which a Receiving Party can prove (1) was lawfully in its possession at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party or (2) was developed by the Receiving Party independently of any Information received from the Disclosing Party and without otherwise violating any of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and not in contravention of applicable law' from (ii) in the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1, we create a contradiction with Section 2(a). Section 1 now states that information known to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis from another source is *not* Confidential Information, regardless of whether that source is legally allowed to disclose it. This directly contradicts Section 2(a), which obligates the Receiving Party to keep *all* Confidential Information secret, even if it was obtained from another source. The phrase 'and not in contravention of applicable law' previously clarified that the Receiving party should ensure the information being disclosed is not violating any confidential agreement with a 3rd party. This ensures that the definition of confidential information is not being 'mis-used'. By deleting this, it suggests confidential information is not confidential and therefore, contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information; Confidentiality ​ . (a) Each Receiving Party shall keep all Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, and shall not use such Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction. The exposure of the Receiving Party to the Confidential Information or its disclosure shall not be construed in any way to grant to the Receiving Party any right or license with respect to the Information other than the right to use such Confidential Information strictly in accordance with this Agreement. Moreover, each Receiving Party shall have the right Confidentiality Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 Page 2 of 6 Rev. 08-Oct-18 to disclose that it is evaluating the Transaction and transmit Confidential Information to its Representatives only if and to the extent that such Representatives need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree (in writing) to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for any actions by its Affiliates and/or Representatives which are not in accordance with the agreements herein regarding the conduct of its Affiliates and/or Representatives. Visitors shall not take photographs or otherwise record the activities at the other party’s facilities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'and not in contravention of applicable law' from the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 2(a), which requires the Receiving Party to keep all Confidential Information secret. The removal now implies that information received non-confidentially from another source is not Confidential Information, even if the source isn't legally allowed to disclose it. This conflicts with Section 2(a)'s obligation to keep *all* Confidential Information confidential, regardless of its source, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information; Confidentiality ​ . (a) Each Receiving Party shall keep all Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, and shall not use such Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction. The exposure of the Receiving Party to the Confidential Information or its disclosure shall not be construed in any way to grant to the Receiving Party any right or license with respect to the Information other than the right to use such Confidential Information strictly in accordance with this Agreement. Moreover, each Receiving Party shall have the right Confidentiality Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 Page 2 of 6 Rev. 08-Oct-18 to disclose that it is evaluating the Transaction and transmit Confidential Information to its Representatives only if and to the extent that such Representatives need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree (in writing) to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for any actions by its Affiliates and/or Representatives which are not in accordance with the agreements herein regarding the conduct of its Affiliates and/or Representatives. Visitors shall not take photographs or otherwise record the activities at the other party’s facilities.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information; Confidentiality ​ . (a) Each Receiving Party shall keep all Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential, *except for information that is publicly available*, and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, and shall not use such Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction. The exposure of the Receiving Party to the Confidential Information or its disclosure shall not be construed in any way to grant to the Receiving Party any right or license with respect to the Information other than the right to use such Confidential Information strictly in accordance with this Agreement. Moreover, each Receiving Party shall have the right to disclose that it is evaluating the Transaction and transmit Confidential Information to its Representatives only if and to the extent that such Representatives need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree (in writing) to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for any actions by its Affiliates and/or Representatives which are not in accordance with the agreements herein regarding the conduct of its Affiliates and/or Representatives. Visitors shall not take photographs or otherwise record the activities at the other party’s facilities.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase, '*except for information that is publicly available*', inserted in Section 2(a) conflicts directly with the original definition of 'Confidential Information' outlined in Section 1(b). Section 1(b) explicitly states that Confidential Information *excludes* information already publicly available. By adding this exception to Section 2(a), it implies that some publicly available information *is* considered Confidential Information, thus creating an in-text contradiction. This amendment creates uncertainty about which information the Receiving Party is obligated to keep confidential, undermining the intent of the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Definitions ​ . The following terms, as used herein, shall have the following meanings: (a) “ ​ Affiliate ​ ” means a Person that directly, or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controls, or is controlled by, or is under common control with, the Person specified of a Party. (b) “ ​ Confidential ​ Information ​ ” means all information, whether visual, written, electronic or oral, (including any information furnished prior to the execution of this Agreement) related to the products, services, technology, business, customers, working capital, affairs, assets, condition (financial or otherwise) or prospects of a Disclosing Party which is furnished by such Disclosing Party, or otherwise disclosed by such Disclosing Party, to a Receiving Party in connection with the consideration and evaluation of the Transaction (including, without limitation, samples, compounds and ingredients thereof, prototypes, formulae, technical know how, technical data, financial information, payment terms, performance data, product specifications and customer and/or supplier information) and all reports, analyses, compilations, data, studies and other materials (in whatever form maintained, whether documentary, computer storage or otherwise) UNITED STATES ​ | 545 W. Lake St. | Elmhurst, IL 60126 | 800.831.7294 MEXICO ​ | Nicolas Gogol #11332-A | Complejo Industrial Chihuahua | Chihuahua, Chih. 31136 www.chiefent.com Confidentiality Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 Page 1 of 6 Rev. 25-Feb-2020 prepared by the Receiving Party containing or reflecting such furnished information. All such Information shall be identified as confidential and/or proprietary in accordance with the following: (i) in the case of written or electronic information, such Information shall be identified at the time of disclosure with an appropriate legend, marking, stamp, or positive written identification on the face thereof or by an accompanying written statement; (ii) in the case of visual information, such Information shall be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or as soon thereafter as is reasonably practicable; and (iii) with respect to oral information, such information shall be reduced to written or language form, marked as confidential with an appropriate legend, marking, stamp, or positive written identification, and transmitted to the Receiving Party within thirty (30) days following the oral disclosure. The term “ ​ Confidential Information ​ ” does not include information (i) which is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Receiving Party or anyone to whom the Receiving Party transmits any Information, (ii) which is or becomes known or available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis and not in contravention of applicable law from a source (other than the Disclosing Party) which has represented to the Receiving Party (which the Receiving Party has no reason to disbelieve after due inquiry) that it is entitled to disclose it to the Receiving Party on such basis or (iii) which a Receiving Party can prove (1) was lawfully in its possession at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party or (2) was developed by the Receiving Party independently of any Information received from the Disclosing Party and without otherwise violating any of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 2(a) adds "except for information that is publicly available". Section 1(b) defines 'Confidential Information' and explicitly states that it *excludes* information already publicly available. By adding this exception to Section 2(a), the changed text implies that some publicly available information *is* considered Confidential Information. This creates a direct conflict and thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Non-Solicitation. The Company acknowledges that Chief Enterprises has invested and will continue to invest significant time and money to develop valuable, continuing relationships with existing and prospective clients and customers of Chief Enterprises. The Company further recognizes that it will obtain valuable Confidential Information about Chief Enterprises customers, clients, suppliers, business partner, and/or vendors, and their business requirements. The Company agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and for two (2) years following its termination, Company will not directly or indirectly, solicit or assist others in soliciting, or attempt to solicit or assist others in attempting to solicit, (i) any existing customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information, or (ii) any prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information and with whom Chief Enterprises took significant steps to do business with during the term of this Agreement, for the purpose or effect of inducing such existing or prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors to cease doing business or reduce their business with Chief Enterprises or to purchase, lease, or utilize products or services that are competitive with, similar to, or that may be used as substitutes for any products or services offered by Chief Enterprises.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Non-Solicitation. The Company acknowledges that Chief Enterprises has invested and will continue to invest significant time and money to develop valuable, continuing relationships with existing and prospective clients and customers of Chief Enterprises. The Company further recognizes that it will obtain valuable Confidential Information about Chief Enterprises customers, clients, suppliers, business partner, and/or vendors, and their business requirements. The Company agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and for two (2) years following its termination, Company will not directly or indirectly, solicit or assist others in soliciting, or attempt to solicit or assist others in attempting to solicit, (i) any existing customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information, or (ii) any prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information and with whom Chief Enterprises took significant steps to do business with during the term of this Agreement, for the purpose or effect of inducing such existing or prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors to cease doing business or reduce their business with Chief Enterprises or to purchase, lease, or utilize products or services that are competitive with, similar to, or that may be used as substitutes for any products or services offered by Chief Enterprises *unless such customers independently seek out the Company*.
perturbation.explanation: By adding '*unless such customers independently seek out the Company*' to the end of section 7, an in-text contradiction is created. Section 7 is for the purpose of Non-Solicitation, that the company agrees to not solicit any customer, client, vendor, etc, for the term of the agreement for the next 2 years following its termination. By adding '*unless such customers independently seek out the Company*', it now creates a possibility that the 'Company' could engage in sales or services with a customer, provided that the customer seeks them out on their own. It causes and in-text contradiction to section 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Non-Solicitation. The Company acknowledges that Chief Enterprises has invested and will continue to invest significant time and money to develop valuable, continuing relationships with existing and prospective clients and customers of Chief Enterprises. The Company further recognizes that it will obtain valuable Confidential Information about Chief Enterprises customers, clients, suppliers, business partner, and/or vendors, and their business requirements. The Company agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and for two (2) years following its termination, Company will not directly or indirectly, solicit or assist others in soliciting, or attempt to solicit or assist others in attempting to solicit, (i) any existing customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information, or (ii) any prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information and with whom Chief Enterprises took significant steps to do business with during the term of this Agreement, for the purpose or effect of inducing such existing or prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors to cease doing business or reduce their business with Chief Enterprises or to purchase, lease, or utilize products or services that are competitive with, similar to, or that may be used as substitutes for any products or services offered by Chief Enterprises *unless such customers independently seek out the Company*.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 7 strictly prohibits the Company from soliciting or assisting in soliciting customers of Chief Enterprises for a specified period. The changed text adds an exception, stating that this prohibition does not apply if the customers independently seek out the Company. This creates a direct contradiction within Section 7 itself (and therefore with the contradicted text which is identical), as it introduces a scenario where the Company can engage with Chief Enterprises' customers, which was previously forbidden, thereby undermining the non-solicitation agreement. The addition of 'unless such customers independently seek out the Company' directly conflicts with the initial agreement that the Company will not solicit those customers, which qualifies it as a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Non-Waiver ​ . No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Non-Waiver ​ . No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, *unless explicitly stated in writing*, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By adding '*unless explicitly stated in writing*' to Section 8, which is for the clause of Non-Waiver, it created an in-text contradiction. This is because it undermines the original intent of a no failure or delay should preclude a waver. It adds a loophole that if the the failure or delay is documented, the waver can be enacted. Now the no-waiver clause, is now, 'sometimes a waver clause'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Non-Waiver ​ . No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, *unless explicitly stated in writing*, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The addition of '*unless explicitly stated in writing*' to Section 8 creates a direct contradiction with the original intent of the non-waiver clause. The original clause states that no failure or delay shall operate as a waiver. The modified clause introduces an exception, essentially stating that a failure or delay *can* operate as a waiver if explicitly stated in writing. This fundamentally alters the meaning of the non-waiver clause and directly conflicts with its initial purpose, as explained by the Perturbation Explanation. The Contradicted Text is the Changed Text itself, further illustrating the self-contradictory nature of the modified clause.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Term and Termination ​ . This Agreement shall be in effect for a period of two (2) years from the date hereof, provided that (i) either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving 60 days’ written notice in accordance with the notice provisions hereof and (ii) with respect to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder, the confidentiality obligations set forth in Paragraph 2(a) shall remain in full force and effect for so long as such Confidential Information remains confidential and non-public. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, information or property which is protected by law for a longer period of time (e.g. trade secrets) shall continue to receive all protections to which it is entitled for so long as it qualifies for protection under the applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Term and Termination ​ . This Agreement shall be in effect for a period of two (2) years from the date hereof, provided that (i) either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving 60 days’ written notice in accordance with the notice provisions hereof *unless both parties mutually agree to forgo the 60 days written notice* and (ii) with respect to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder, the confidentiality obligations set forth in Paragraph 2(a) shall remain in full force and effect for so long as such Confidential Information remains confidential and non-public. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, information or property which is protected by law for a longer period of time (e.g. trade secrets) shall continue to receive all protections to which it is entitled for so long as it qualifies for protection under the applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: By inserting the phrase '*unless both parties mutually agree to forgo the 60 days written notice*' in Section 15, an in-text contradiction is made. The original section states that either party may terminate the agreement, but must give 60 days written notice, to uphold the contract and terms. By adding '*unless both parties mutually agree to forgo the 60 days written notice*', creates an in-text contradiction because either party may terminate the agreement WITHOUT following the 60 days written notice. Now Section 15's original clause to give 60 days written notice, is undermined, and makes the section itself an in-text contradictory clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. Term and Termination ​ . This Agreement shall be in effect for a period of two (2) years from the date hereof, provided that (i) either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving 60 days’ written notice in accordance with the notice provisions hereof *unless both parties mutually agree to forgo the 60 days written notice* and (ii) with respect to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder, the confidentiality obligations set forth in Paragraph 2(a) shall remain in full force and effect for so long as such Confidential Information remains confidential and non-public. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, information or property which is protected by law for a longer period of time (e.g. trade secrets) shall continue to receive all protections to which it is entitled for so long as it qualifies for protection under the applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 15 to allow termination without the originally required 60-day written notice if both parties agree. This directly contradicts the initial provision in the same section (contradicted text) mandating a 60-day written notice for termination, thereby creating a clear and strong in-text contradiction. The added clause provides an exception that undermines the original obligation, creating inconsistency and uncertainty about the termination process.
